title: Toolformer: Language Models Can Teach Themselves to Use Tools
reward_signal: likelihood-based API call filtering during data construction
tool_use_setting: simple APIs such as calculator, QA, search, translation, and calendar tools
notes: Early tool-use training reference; not RL training, but directly relevant to deciding when and how to call APIs.

title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
reward_signal: environment/task feedback used for evaluation rather than policy training
tool_use_setting: interleaved reasoning traces and actions in knowledge and decision-making environments
notes: Representative inference-time agent baseline; useful for trajectory and action-observation framing.

title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
reward_signal: scalar or verbal feedback converted into reflection memory
tool_use_setting: sequential decision-making, coding, and reasoning tasks
notes: Title uses reinforcement-learning language, but the core mechanism is verbal feedback and memory rather than gradient RL over model weights.

title: ToolLLM: Facilitating Large Language Models to Master 16000+ Real-world APIs
training_paradigm: SFT / tool-learning data construction / benchmark-only evaluation
reward_signal: tool-use success metrics and model-based ToolEval; no core RL reward reported
tool_use_setting: real-world REST APIs from RapidAPI via ToolBench
notes: Core representative benchmark/data/training reference for open-source API-use agents.

title: API-Bank: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Tool-Augmented LLMs
reward_signal: API-call correctness and task completion evaluation
tool_use_setting: tool-augmented LLMs with API calls
notes: Prompt used APIBench; canonical paper found as API-Bank. Keep naming conflict tracked.

title: AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents
reward_signal: environment-specific task success metrics
tool_use_setting: OS, database, knowledge graph, digital game, web browsing, and other agent environments
notes: Useful for failure-mode and long-horizon evaluation taxonomy; do not label as training method.

title: WebArena: A Realistic Web Environment for Building Autonomous Agents
reward_signal: end-to-end functional task success
tool_use_setting: realistic websites plus tools and external knowledge bases
notes: Key long-horizon web-agent benchmark for semantic success versus surface action success.

title: SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues?
reward_signal: repository test pass/fail and issue resolution
tool_use_setting: real GitHub issues requiring code edits and execution environments
notes: Important execution-based reward/evaluation analogue; benchmark-only unless discussing SWE-Llama baseline separately.

title: tau-bench: A Benchmark for Tool-Agent-User Interaction in Real-World Domains
reward_signal: domain policy compliance and task completion across simulated user interactions
tool_use_setting: customer-service style domains with API tools and policy guidelines
notes: Central multi-turn tool-agent-user benchmark for API transaction reward design.

title: ToRA: A Tool-Integrated Reasoning Agent for Mathematical Problem Solving
reward_signal: answer correctness and tool-interaction evaluation; no RL reward in core method
tool_use_setting: math reasoning with computation libraries and symbolic solvers
benchmark: MATH and other math reasoning datasets
notes: Useful for tool-integrated reasoning and execution-feedback analogies, but not agentic RL training.

title: AgentTuning: Enabling Generalized Agent Abilities for LLMs
reward_signal: agent task benchmark metrics; no RL reward in core method
tool_use_setting: mixed agent tasks collected as AgentInstruct
notes: Representative agent fine-tuning baseline; classify separately from RL.

title: FireAct: Toward Language Agent Fine-tuning
reward_signal: QA task performance and trajectory quality; no RL reward in core method
tool_use_setting: question answering with Google Search API and ReAct-style trajectories
notes: Representative fine-tuning of language agents with tool trajectories.

title: xLAM: A Family of Large Action Models to Empower AI Agent Systems
reward_signal: function-calling benchmark accuracy
tool_use_setting: tool and function calling across agent benchmarks, including BFCL
benchmark: BFCL and other agent ability benchmarks
notes: Important function-calling model family; reward details need deeper extraction from paper.

title: ToolACE: Winning the Points of LLM Function Calling
reward_signal: tool-learning data quality and function-calling benchmark performance
tool_use_setting: function calling and tool-learning data pipeline
notes: Automatic agentic pipeline for tool-learning data; not verified as RL training.

title: APIGen: Automated Pipeline for Generating Verifiable and Diverse Function-Calling Datasets
reward_signal: format checking, actual function execution, and semantic verification
tool_use_setting: executable APIs and generated function-calling data
notes: Highly relevant to separating format, execution, and semantic verification signals.

title: APIGen-MT: Agentic Pipeline for Multi-Turn Data Generation via Simulated Agent-Human Interplay
reward_signal: verifiable multi-turn tool-use data checks
tool_use_setting: simulated agent-human interplay for multi-turn API/tool use
notes: Second-round 2025 source for multi-turn data construction; needs deeper extraction.

title: ReTool: Reinforcement Learning for Strategic Tool Use in LLMs
reward_signal: task outcome rewards for tool invocation strategy
tool_use_setting: long-form reasoning with code/tool execution interleaved with natural language reasoning
notes: Key 2025 agentic RL/tool-use source; name conflicts with the commercial Retool platform.

title: Agentic Reasoning and Tool Integration for LLMs via Reinforcement Learning
reward_signal: outcome-based RL for multi-turn reasoning chains and tool/environment interaction
tool_use_setting: math reasoning and multi-turn function calling with dynamic tool selection
notes: ARTIST acronym resolved as Agentic Reasoning and Tool Integration in Self-improving Transformers; older unrelated artist-agent RL work exists.

title: RAGEN: Understanding Self-Evolution in LLM Agents via Multi-Turn Reinforcement Learning
reward_signal: state-thinking-actions-reward trajectory-level agent reward
tool_use_setting: modular training and evaluation system for multi-turn LLM agents
notes: Important for multi-turn RL stability, rollout diversity, and self-evolution analysis.

title: Search-R1: Training LLMs to Reason and Leverage Search Engines with Reinforcement Learning
reward_signal: answer correctness and structured search/reasoning behavior rewards
tool_use_setting: LLM interacts with search engines during reasoning
notes: Key search-tool RL source for 2025 update.

title: ToolRL: Reward is All Tool Learning Needs
reward_signal: tool-learning reward signal; details require extraction
tool_use_setting: LLM tool learning
notes: Canonical arXiv source found; reward details need full-paper extraction in Prompt 4.

title: The Berkeley Function Calling Leaderboard (BFCL): From Tool Use to Agentic Evaluation of Large Language Models
reward_signal: function name matching, required parameter matching, type/value matching, executable function-call evaluation, relevance detection
tool_use_setting: single, parallel, multiple, executable, REST, SQL, and multi-turn function calling
notes: Use official Berkeley/Gorilla leaderboard plus paper; distinguish benchmark versions in later audit.

title: Process Reward Models for LLM Agents: Practical Framework and Directions
reward_signal: step-wise process reward shaping and model-predictive reasoning signals
tool_use_setting: LLM agents improving through interactions
notes: Core process-reward source for LLM agents; separate from later similarly named AgentPRM paper.

title: Direct Multi-Turn Preference Optimization for Language Agents
reward_signal: trajectory-level preference pairs optimized with direct preference objective
tool_use_setting: multi-turn language agents
notes: Directly addresses DPO-style optimization for language agents and compounding errors.

title: EPO: Hierarchical LLM Agents with Environment Preference Optimization
reward_signal: preference signals generated from multimodal environment feedback
tool_use_setting: long-horizon embodied agent tasks with environment feedback
notes: Adjacent but relevant environment-feedback preference optimization source; not tool/API-specific.

title: Applying RLAIF for Code Generation with API-usage in Lightweight LLMs
reward_signal: AI feedback for code generation with API usage
tool_use_setting: lightweight LLMs generating code that uses APIs
notes: Best first-pass match for RLAIF plus API usage; narrower than full agentic tool-use.

title: CM2: Reinforcement Learning with Checklist Rewards for Multi-Turn and Multi-Step Agentic Tool Use
reward_signal: checklist rewards over multi-turn and multi-step tool-use behavior
tool_use_setting: agentic tool use with simulated and recorded tool environments
notes: Newest high-relevance 2026 source for dense checklist rewards in tool-use RL.

title: MUA-RL: Multi-turn User-interacting Agent Reinforcement Learning for agentic tool use
reward_signal: user-interaction and tool-use task rewards from simulated users
tool_use_setting: agentic tool use with LLM-simulated users inside the RL loop
notes: Important 2025 multi-turn RL direction; needs full-paper extraction.

title: Reinforcing Multi-Turn Reasoning in LLM Agents via Turn-Level Credit Assignment
task_type: multi-turn reasoning and search tool-use RL
training_paradigm: GRPO / PPO with turn-level credit assignment
reward_signal: turn-level rewards for multi-turn reasoning and search-based tool-use tasks
tool_use_setting: multi-turn reasoning agents and search-based tool-use tasks
notes: Relevant to cascade drift and sparse reward credit assignment.

title: Training Task Reasoning LLM Agents for Multi-turn Task Planning via Single-turn Reinforcement Learning
reward_signal: dense and verifiable reward from expert trajectories
tool_use_setting: task reasoning for autonomous agents with tool-use-relevant planning
notes: Relevant GRPO source, though the task-planning setting needs extraction before core inclusion.

title: ToolPRMBench: Evaluating and Advancing Process Reward Models for Tool-using Agents
reward_signal: step-level labels and process reward model evaluation for tool-using trajectories
tool_use_setting: representative tool-using benchmarks converted into step-level PRM cases
notes: Key 2026 process reward benchmark for tool-using agents; useful for Prompt 4 extraction.

title: R1-Searcher: Incentivizing the Search Capability in LLMs via Reinforcement Learning
reward_signal: outcome-based search capability reward
tool_use_setting: LLM search behavior
notes: Adjacent search-RL source; compare with Search-R1 during extraction.

title: Synthetic Data Generation and Multi-Step RL for Reasoning and Tool Use
reward_signal: per-step generative reward model over synthetic multi-step trajectories
tool_use_setting: multi-step reasoning and tool-use trajectories
notes: Relevant to process rewards and step-wise RL; metadata needs primary-page extraction.
